2604.18401.title: StepPO: Step-Aligned Policy Optimization for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2604.18401.paper_summary: General agents have given rise to phenomenal applications such as OpenClaw and Claude Code. As these agent systems (a.k.a. Harnesses) strive for bolder goals, they demand increasingly stronger agentic capabilities from foundation Large Language Models (LLMs). Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) is emerging as a central post-training paradigm for empowering LLMs with these capabilities and is playing an increasingly pivotal role in agent training. Unlike single-turn token-level alignment or reasoning enhancement, as in RLHF and RLVR, Agentic RL targets multi-turn interactive settings, where the goal is to optimize core agentic capabilities such as decision making and tool use while addressing new challenges including delayed and sparse rewards, as well as long and variable context. As a result, the token-centric modeling and optimization paradigm inherited from traditional LLM RL is becoming increasingly inadequate for capturing real LLM agent behavior. In this paper, we present StepPO as a position on step-level Agentic RL. We argue that the conventional token-level Markov Decision Process (MDP) should be advanced to a step-level MDP formulation, and that the step, rather than the token, should be regarded as the proper action representation for LLM agents. We then propose step-level credit assignment as the natural optimization counterpart of this formulation, thereby aligning policy optimization and reward propagation with the granularity of agent decisions. Finally, we discuss the key systems designs required to realize step-level Agentic RL in practice and preliminary experiments provide initial evidence for the effectiveness of this perspective. We hope that the step-aligned, step-level paradigm embodied in StepPO offers the Agentic RL community a useful lens for understanding agent behavior and helps advance LLMs toward stronger general-agent capabilities.
2604.18401.paper_summary_zh: 一般代理人催生了如 OpenClaw 和 Claude Code 等驚人的應用。隨著這些代理系統（即 Harnesses）追求更大膽的目標，它們對基礎大型語言模型（LLMs）提出了越來越強的代理能力需求。代理強化學習（RL）正在成為賦予 LLM 這些能力的中心後訓練範式，並在代理訓練中扮演著越來越關鍵的角色。與單回合的標記級對齊或推理增強（如 RLHF 和 RLVR）不同，代理 RL 針對多回合互動環境，目標是優化核心代理能力，如決策和工具使用，同時應對包括延遲和稀疏獎勵以及長期和可變上下文等新挑戰。因此，從傳統 LLM RL 繼承的以標記為中心的建模和優化範式越來越無法捕捉真實的 LLM 代理行為。在本文中，我們提出 StepPO 作為步驟級代理 RL 的一種立場。我們認為，傳統的標記級馬爾可夫決策過程（MDP）應該推進到步驟級 MDP 的公式化，並且步驟，而不是標記，應被視為 LLM 代理的適當行動表示。然後，我們提出步驟級信用分配作為這一公式化的自然優化對應，從而使政策優化和獎勵傳播與代理決策的粒度對齊。最後，我們討論了實現步驟級代理 RL 所需的關鍵系統設計，初步實驗提供了這一觀點有效性的初步證據。我們希望 StepPO 中體現的步驟對齊、步驟級範式能為代理 RL 社群提供一個有用的視角，以理解代理行為，並幫助推進 LLM 向更強的通用代理能力邁進。
2604.18401.author: Daoyu Wang et.al.
2604.18401.authors: Daoyu Wang, Qingchuan Li, Mingyue Cheng, Jie Ouyang, Shuo Yu, Qi Liu, Enhong Chen
